title: Piquant Aerated Canadien Tomato Tagine
description: A refreshing and light Canadian dish featuring aerated tomatoes in a confit sauce, perfect for a monsoon evening. This recipe is low in calories and features canola oil for a healthier twist.

ingredients.name: Diced fresh tomatoes
ingredients.notes: canned or fresh, but avoid cherry tomatoes.

ingredients.name: Confited tomato puree
ingredients.notes: homemade or store-bought, preferably without added salt.

instructions: Heat the canola oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the diced fresh tomatoes and cook until they start to release their juices, about 5 minutes.
Add the confited tomato puree, fennel seeds, cumin, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While the sauce simmers, prepare an ice bath by filling a large bowl with ice and water.
Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into the ice bath, and let it cool to room temperature.
Once the sauce has cooled, use a hand blender or a regular blender to puree it until smooth.
Return the sauce to the saucepan and reheat it over low heat, stirring occasionally, until it's warm and bubbly.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the Piquant Aerated Canadien Tomato Tagine over cooked basmati rice or with some crusty bread, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Garnish with edible flowers or microgreens if you like a pop of color.
Let the tagine sit at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before refrigerating or freezing it for later use.
